skill.name: context7-auto-research
skill.description: Automatically fetch latest library/framework documentation for Claude Code via Context7 API
skill.summary: Automatically fetch latest library/framework documentation for Claude Code via Context7 API
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static external command and network findings are real documentation patterns, but no automatic execution or runtime network request is present in SKILL.md. The weak cryptography findings at lines 3 and 33 are false positives in descriptive prose. The remaining medium concern is supply-chain context because the marketplace source references sickn33, while install and example links point to BenedictKing.

medium_findings.title: External Installation Reference Requires Verification
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: SKILL.md includes an npx install command and a GitHub example link that point to BenedictKing/context7-auto-research while the marketplace source is sickn33. This is not executed automatically, but users should verify the external repository before running the command.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced command and URL are visible in the skill file, and the owner mismatch is clear from the report metadata. Confidence is below very high because the text is documentation and does not prove malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The static weak cryptography hits at lines 3 and 33 occur in descriptive text and repository guidance. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing implementation, or encryption code appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain plain documentation text, not code. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or another weak cryptographic algorithm in SKILL.md.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Install Command In Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE POSITIVE as a static pattern: the file contains an npx command in a fenced bash block. It is an installation instruction rather than code executed by the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The bash block and npx command are directly present. The risk is limited because the command is not run automatically by the skill file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded External Repository Link
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE POSITIVE as a static pattern: the file links to a GitHub repository for examples. No evidence found that this URL is fetched automatically or used to exfiltrate data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is directly present on the cited line. The semantic context is a documentation link, so it is not a confirmed runtime network behavior.

content.value_statement: AI coding sessions often use stale library knowledge when APIs change quickly. This skill prompts Claude Code to fetch current framework documentation through Context7 before giving implementation advice.
actual_capabilities: Prompts the assistant to use current documentation for libraries and frameworks.
Calls out React, Next.js, Prisma, and other popular libraries as target topics.
Describes Context7 API usage for retrieving library and framework documentation.
Documents optional API key configuration through environment variables.
Provides installation and repository references for examples and troubleshooting.
limitations: The skill file contains guidance only and no local implementation code.
Context7 availability and API configuration are handled outside this skill file.
The documented install command points to an external GitHub repository that users should verify.
No offline documentation cache or fallback behavior is documented.

use_cases.title: Research a React API Change
use_cases.description: Check current React documentation before generating component guidance or migration steps.

use_cases.title: Verify Next.js Framework Behavior
use_cases.description: Use recent framework documentation before answering routing, rendering, or configuration questions.

use_cases.title: Ground ORM Advice in Current Docs
use_cases.description: Review current Prisma documentation before suggesting schema, query, or migration patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Check Current Docs
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one library question.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use current documentation before answering. How should I use this library feature today?

prompt_templates.title: Compare Framework Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request across two documented features.
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch current docs for the relevant framework features, then compare the supported approaches and tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an Upgrade
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced migration planning with changed APIs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research the latest documentation for this package and create an upgrade plan with breaking changes and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Existing Guidance
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of prior assistant output.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check current library documentation, then review this implementation advice for outdated APIs or unsupported assumptions.

output_examples.input: How should I handle data fetching in the current Next.js version?
output_examples.output: The response first checks current Next.js documentation, then summarizes the recommended patterns and version-sensitive caveats.

output_examples.input: Review my Prisma migration plan for outdated steps.
output_examples.output: The response identifies which migration steps match current Prisma guidance and flags any steps that need verification.

output_examples.input: Is this React hook pattern still recommended?
output_examples.output: The response compares the pattern against current React documentation and explains whether it remains supported.

best_practices: Ask for current documentation when using fast-changing libraries or frameworks.
Configure API keys through environment variables when higher rate limits are needed.
Verify external repository links before running installation commands from documentation.
anti_patterns: Do not treat retrieved documentation as a substitute for project-specific testing.
Do not run external install commands without checking the repository and package source.
Do not use this skill for private documentation that Context7 cannot access.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps Claude Code ground library and framework answers in current Context7 documentation.

faq.question: Does it include implementation code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill file contains usage guidance, installation notes, and links.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: The skill says API key configuration is optional and useful for higher rate limits.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is the external command safe?
faq.answer: It is not executed automatically, but users should verify the referenced repository before running it.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill contains external installation and repository references, including an owner mismatch that needs user verification.
